task: Aligns the completed Companion Platform with the new VihuPlanet Companion Canon -- deliberately a canon-alignment and asset-registration sprint, not a runtime sprint: js/companionEngine.js was NOT touched, not one line (verified via git diff). Canon 1 (Story Egg, representing every Visitor -- no face, no limbs, never speaks, poses only: hero/idle/curious/thinking/excited/sleep/hatching) and Canon 2 (Lumo, the Guardian of Story Companions, owned by VihuPlanet, never claimable -- poses: hero/idle/wave/curious/think/celebrate/sleep, retiring the pre-canon 'talk' pose) are frozen in a new docs/COMPANION_CANON.md. Story Egg is registered as a real canonical platform entity (assets/companions/story-egg/companion.json + animations.json, both real) with its 7 PNGs genuinely still pending upload -- disclosed, not fabricated, per explicit instruction not to generate replacement art. Lumo's companion.json/animations.json/personality.json were updated to match Canon 2 exactly (hero added as a real state, talk retired and talk.png deleted, role updated to 'Guardian of Story Companions'); registry.json gained a role field per entry (guardian/visitor) and a second entry for story-egg. js/companionDirector.js was rewritten around one small, data-driven MODES table (visitor/creator) -- Visitor vs. Creator is read from MagicCard.getActive() (Studio's own existing Magic Card Identity Evolution state, unmodified), and which registry entity actually loads is resolved by matching MODES[mode].role against each registry entry's own role field, never a literal id branch (exactly one disclosed 'lumo' fallback literal remains, for when the registry itself is unreachable). Visitor mode boots the Story Egg with zero speech bubbles ever (structural, via MODES.visitor.speaks:false, never dependent on Story Egg simply lacking a personality.json) and its own pose vocabulary for every existing Studio event (typing/creating/artwork/publish); Creator mode keeps Lumo's exact pre-existing wave+greeting/think/celebrate/sleep choreography unchanged. A new notify('creator-born') event (hooked into js/magicCard.js's claim() and adopt() -- the two real Visitor-becomes-Creator moments) swaps the active entity from Story Egg to Lumo via the engine's own frozen unload()+load() pair on the same widget, running Lumo's boot choreography immediately -- invisible until the Magic Card overlay closes, via the pre-existing, unmodified dialog-occlusion watcher. A new notify('ceremony-closed') event (hooked into js/magicCardUI.js's _finishAwakening(), the ceremony's one true exit point regardless of outcome) settles a Story Egg left mid-hatching back to idle when the ceremony closes without a claim. Two real, disclosed bugs were found and fixed while tracing this exact path: CompanionDirector.init()'s call site moved from unconditional top-level boot into js/app.js's _beginBoot() (which only runs after the Magic Card Identity Gate resolves), since mode detection needs the gate's own outcome (a specific card pick, or Begin Exploring) to be settled first, not a stale snapshot from page-load time; and js/magicCardUI.js's proceed(cardId) only ever called MagicCard.setActive() when a real id was chosen, silently leaving a previous session's stale active-card id in place when 'Begin Exploring' was picked on a shared device -- fixed to unconditionally call setActive(cardId||null), which already correctly clears the pointer. Verified via a new 40-assertion Playwright suite (static diff/reference checks, full Visitor and Creator choreography including the no-speech-ever guarantee, the creator-born entity swap, ceremony-closed settling, and a real end-to-end drive of the two-card Identity Gate through 'Begin Exploring' proving both the timing fix and the active-id bugfix) plus the full existing 12-script Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright regression suite and both goldenBuild.js suites -- all passing unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change, no Companion Runtime/Registry-mechanism/Package-Contract redesign; confined to companionDirector.js, three Magic Card integration hook sites, Lumo's three data files, the new Story Egg package's two data files, registry.json, and documentation.
